metadata.name: numerous-widgets
metadata.summary: Widgets and patterns for building apps using frameworks like Panel, Marimo, and Jupyter based on the AnyWidget library.

classes.name: numerous.widgets.base.markdown_drawer.MarkdownDrawer
documentation:
A collapsible drawer widget that displays markdown content.

Args:
    title: The title shown in the drawer header
    content: The markdown content to display
    open: Whether the drawer starts open (default: False)

classes.name: numerous.widgets.base.checkbox.CheckBox

methods.name: numerous.widgets.base.checkbox.CheckBox.selected_value
documentation: Returns the current checkbox state.

A widget for selecting a boolean value.

The selected value can be accessed via the `selected_value` property.

Args:
    label: The label of the checkbox.
    tooltip: The tooltip of the checkbox.
    default: The default value of the checkbox.

functions.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.dict_hash
documentation:
Approximate a hash for a dictionary assuming:
- Keys are strings.
- Values are hashable.

functions.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.compare_with_dict
documentation:
Compare two values or dictionaries recursively.

Args:
    d1: The first value or dictionary to compare.
    d2: The second value or dictionary to compare.

Returns:
    True if the values or dictionaries are equal, False otherwise.

classes.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.Input
documentation:
A class representing an input to the state handler.

Args:
    name: The name of the input.
    get_value: A callable that returns the current value of the input.
    set_value: A callable that sets the value of the input.

classes.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.Output
documentation:
A class representing an output to the state handler.

Args:
    name: The name of the output.
    get_value: A callable that returns the current value of the output.
    set_value: A callable that sets the value of the output.

classes.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.StateHandler

methods.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.StateHandler.register_input
documentation:
Register an input to the state handler.

Args:
    name: The name of the input.
    get_value: A callable that returns the current value of the input.
    set_value: A callable that sets the value of the input.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.StateHandler.register_output
documentation:
Register an output to the state handler.

Args:
    name: The name of the output.
    get_value: A callable that returns the current value of the output.
    set_value: A callable that sets the value of the output.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.StateHandler.roll_back_inputs
documentation:
Roll back the inputs to a previous commit.

Args:
    commit_id: The commit id to roll back to.
    inputs: The inputs to roll back. If None, all inputs are rolled back.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.StateHandler.roll_back_outputs
documentation:
Roll back the outputs to a previous commit.

Args:
    commit_id: The commit id to roll back to.
    outputs: The outputs to roll back. If None, all outputs are rolled back.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.StateHandler.roll_back
documentation:
Roll back the inputs and outputs to a previous commit.

Args:
    commit_id: The commit id to roll back to.
    inputs: The inputs to roll back. If None, all inputs are rolled back.
    outputs: The outputs to roll back. If None, all outputs are rolled back.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.StateHandler.get_inputs
documentation:
Get the inputs at a previous commit.

Args:
    commit_id: The commit id to get the inputs at.
    inputs: The inputs to get. If None, all inputs are returned.

Returns:
    A dictionary of the inputs at the commit.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.StateHandler.commit_outputs
documentation:
Commit the outputs to a new commit.

Args:
    commit_id: The commit id to commit to.
    output_values: The outputs to commit.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.StateHandler.inputs_valid_for_commit
documentation:
Check if the inputs are valid for a commit.

Args:
    commit_id: The commit id to check.
    inputs: The inputs to check. If None, all inputs are checked.

Returns:
    True if the inputs are valid for the commit, False otherwise.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.StateHandler.outputs_valid_for_commit
documentation:
Check if the outputs are valid for a commit.

Args:
    commit_id: The commit id to check.
    outputs: The outputs to check. If None, all outputs are checked.

Returns:
    True if the outputs are valid for the commit, False otherwise.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.StateHandler.valid_for_commit
documentation:
Check if the inputs and outputs are valid for a commit.

Args:
    commit_id: The commit id to check.
    inputs: The inputs to check. If None, all inputs are checked.
    outputs: The outputs to check. If None, all outputs are checked.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.StateHandler.set_inputs
documentation:
Set the inputs to a new commit.

Args:
    inputs: The inputs to set.
    commit_id: The commit id to set the inputs to.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.StateHandler.set_outputs
documentation:
Set the outputs to a new commit.

Args:
    outputs: The outputs to set.
    commit_id: The commit id to set the outputs to.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.StateHandler.get_outputs
documentation:
Get the outputs at a previous commit.

Args:
    commit_id: The commit id to get the outputs at.
    outputs: The outputs to get. If None, all outputs are returned.

Returns:
    A dictionary of the outputs at the commit.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.StateHandler.get_combined
documentation:
Get the combined inputs and outputs at a previous commit.

Args:
    commit_id: The commit id to get the combined inputs and outputs at.
    inputs: The inputs to get. If None, all inputs are returned.
    outputs: The outputs to get. If None, all outputs are returned.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.StateHandler.from_dict
documentation:
Set the state from a dictionary.

Args:
    dict_data: The dictionary to set the state from.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.state._handler.StateHandler.to_dict
documentation:
Convert the state to a dictionary.

Args:
    commit_id: The commit id to get the dictionary at.
    inputs: The inputs to get. If None, all inputs are returned.
    outputs: The outputs to get. If None, all outputs are returned.

Returns:
    A dictionary of the state at the commit.

A class representing a state handler.

Args:
    inputs: A dictionary of inputs.
    outputs: A dictionary of outputs.

classes.name: numerous.widgets.base.map_selector.MapSelector

methods.name: numerous.widgets.base.map_selector.MapSelector.selected_value
documentation:
Returns the currently selected point ID.

Returns:

class_variables.name: numerous.widgets.base.map_selector.MapSelector.center
documentation: [lon, lat]

class_variables.name: numerous.widgets.base.map_selector.MapSelector.location_clicked
documentation: [lon, lat] of last click

A widget for selecting a point on a map.

The widget is initialized with a dictionary of points, where each point is a tuple of longitude and latitude.
The widget also has a center and zoom level, which can be specified at initialization.

The selected point can be accessed via the `selected_value` property.

The last location clicked can be accessed via the `location_clicked` property.

Args:
    points: A dictionary of points, where each point is a tuple of longitude and latitude.
    center: The center of the map, as a tuple of longitude and latitude.
    zoom: The zoom level of the map.

class_variables.name: numerous.widgets.base.task.Task.error
documentation: Will store error details

A widget for controlling and displaying task progress.

The widget shows a play button that transforms into a stop button with
circular progress indicator when running, and either a checkmark (success)
or X (failure) when finished.

functions.name: numerous.widgets.task.process_task.sync_with_task
documentation:
Synchronize the task widget with the process task

This function synchronizes the task widget with the process task by updating the progress, logs, and error state.

Args:
    task_widget (TaskWidget): The task widget to synchronize
    process_task (ProcessTask): The process task to synchronize with

functions.name: numerous.widgets.task.process_task.process_task_control
documentation:
Control a process task with a task widget

This function creates a task widget and a timer to synchronize the task widget with the process task.

Args:
    process_task (ProcessTask): The process task to control
    on_start (Callable): Callback function for when the task starts
    on_stop (Callable): Callback function for when the task stops
    update_interval (float): The interval between syncs in seconds

Returns:
    Tuple[TaskWidget, Timer]: A tuple containing the task widget and timer

classes.name: numerous.widgets.task.process_task.ProcessTask

methods.name: numerous.widgets.task.process_task.ProcessTask._run_wrapper
documentation:
Internal wrapper method to handle task execution and exception handling.

Args:
    *args: Variable length argument list to pass to run().
    **kwargs: Arbitrary keyword arguments to pass to run().

methods.name: numerous.widgets.task.process_task.ProcessTask.log
documentation:
Add a log entry to the queue.

Args:
    message (str): The message to add to the log queue.

documentation:
Get all accumulated log messages.

Returns:
    str: A string containing all formatted log messages, joined by newlines.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.task.process_task.ProcessTask.run
documentation: Override this method in subclasses to define the simulation.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.task.process_task.ProcessTask.start
documentation:
Start the task in a new process or in the same thread.

Args:
    *args: Variable length argument list to pass to run().
    **kwargs: Arbitrary keyword arguments to pass to run().

methods.name: numerous.widgets.task.process_task.ProcessTask.stop
documentation: Stop the running task forcefully.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.task.process_task.ProcessTask.progress
documentation:
Get the current progress of the task.

Returns:
    float: Progress value between 0.0 and 1.0.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.task.process_task.ProcessTask.result
documentation:
Get the result of the task execution.

Returns:
    Any: The return value from the run() method.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: If an exception occurred during task execution.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.task.process_task.ProcessTask.exception
documentation:
Get any exception that occurred during task execution.

Returns:
    Optional[Union[Tuple[Exception, str], Tuple[datetime, Exception, str]]]: 
        A tuple containing (timestamp, exception, traceback) if available,
        or (exception, traceback) if using cached values,
        or None if no exception occurred.

methods.name: numerous.widgets.task.process_task.ProcessTask.reset
documentation: Reset the task's state to initial conditions.

A base class for running long-running tasks in a separate process with progress tracking.

This class provides functionality for running tasks asynchronously, monitoring their progress,
capturing output, and handling exceptions.

Args:
    stop_message (str): Message to display when the task is forcefully terminated.
        Defaults to "Process was forcefully terminated."
    capture_stdout (bool): Whether to capture stdout/stderr output.
        Defaults to False.
    run_in_process (bool): Whether to run the task in a separate process.
        Defaults to True.

Attributes:
    stop_message (str): Message displayed when task is terminated.
    capture_stdout (bool): Whether stdout/stderr capture is enabled.
    run_in_process (bool): Whether to run the task in a separate process.

classes.name: numerous.widgets.task.process_task.SubprocessTask

documentation:
Initialize the subprocess task.

Args:
    progress_parser: Callable that takes a stdout line and returns a float progress value (0-1) 
                   or None if the line doesn't contain progress info
    *args: Additional arguments passed to ProcessTask
    **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments passed to ProcessTask

methods.name: numerous.widgets.task.process_task.SubprocessTask.run
documentation: Run a shell command and capture its output.

ProcessTask subclass for running shell commands using subprocess.

Captures stdout and stderr, with progress parsing from stdout.

classes.name: numerous.widgets.task.process_task.Simulation

documentation:
Run the simulation.

Args:
    duration (float): The duration of the simulation in seconds.
    input1 (float): Initial value for y.
    input2 (float): Power input value.

Returns:
    tuple: A tuple containing (str, list, list):
        - Status message
        - Time points list
        - Result values list

Example simulation class demonstrating Task usage.

A simple simulation that calculates changes in a value over time based on
power input and loss factors.
